## 1. Mag Interactive
**Location:** Stockholm, Sweden  
**Size:** 51-200 employees  
**Focus:** Word games, casual puzzle (Wordzee, WordBrain, Ruzzle)  
**Funding:** IPO (Nasdaq First North), profitable since 2020  
**Key Contact:** Daniel Hasselberg, CEO & Co-Founder  
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/danielhasselberg  
**Verification:** High (company website, multiple sources)

**Live Ops Focus:** Daily tournaments, seasonal events, ad monetization optimization

**Outreach Angle:**  
> Daniel—Mag Interactive's daily tournament model for Wordzee keeps retention high but requires constant content. We've built AI that generates themed word packs and seasonal event assets—could reduce your content team's manual workload by 70%. Worth a brief conversation about automating live ops content?

---

## 2. Peak Games
**Location:** Istanbul, Turkey  
**Size:** 201-500 employees  
**Focus:** Card games, puzzle (Toy Blast, Toon Blast)  
**Funding:** Acquired by Zynga for $1.8B (2020), now part of Take-Two  
**Key Contact:** Sidar Sahin, Founder & CEO  
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/sidarsahin  
**Verification:** High (public acquisition, press coverage)

**Live Ops Focus:** Daily rewards, limited-time events, level progression systems

**Outreach Angle:**  
> Sidar—Peak's puzzle games are retention machines, but Toon Blast's 6000+ levels required massive art production. We're building AI that generates level layouts and art variations from templates—imagine cutting level design time from weeks to days. Curious if this fits Peak's content pipeline?

---

## 3. Me2Zen
**Location:** Hong Kong / Global (offices in China, Japan, US)  
**Size:** 51-200 employees  
**Focus:** Solitaire, card games, casual casino  
**Funding:** Private, profitable  
**Key Contact:** Alan Fong, CEO & Co-Founder  
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/alanfonghk  
**Verification:** Medium (LinkedIn, company registry)

**Live Ops Focus:** Daily challenges, tournament modes, seasonal themes

**Outreach Angle:**  
> Alan—Me2Zen dominates solitaire with Solitaire Grand Harvest and daily live ops that drive LTV. The challenge: generating fresh card backs, backgrounds, and seasonal themes at scale. We've built AI that auto-generates art variations and card designs—could 10x your content library without scaling the art team. Worth exploring?

## 4. Suji Games
**Location:** Istanbul, Turkey  
**Size:** 11-50 employees  
**Focus:** Hyper-casual, simulation (Texting Story, Operate Now)  
**Funding:** Bootstrapped, profitable  
**Key Contact:** Firat Uslu, CEO & Founder  
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/firatuslu  
**Verification:** Medium (LinkedIn, company website)

**Live Ops Focus:** Content updates for simulation games, seasonal events

**Outreach Angle:**  
> Firat—Suji Games' simulation titles like Operate Now require steady content updates to maintain DAU. We're building AI that generates medical scenarios, dialogue trees, and UI assets—imagine 50 new surgery levels generated per week instead of 5. Could this accelerate your content roadmap?

## 5. Ruby Games (Rovio)
**Location:** Izmir, Turkey  
**Size:** 11-50 employees (part of Rovio)  
**Focus:** Hyper-casual, arcade (Hair Challenge, Shoe Race)  
**Funding:** Acquired by Rovio (2021), now SEGA  
**Key Contact:** Mert Can Kurum, CEO & Founder  
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/mert-can-kurum  
**Verification:** High (Rovio press release, LinkedIn)

**Live Ops Focus:** Rapid content iteration, viral hit chasing

**Outreach Angle:**  
> Mert—Ruby Games' viral hits (Hair Challenge, Shoe Race) prove you understand hyper-casual trends. The challenge: replicating that success at scale. We've built AI that analyzes trending mechanics and generates playable prototypes—could help Ruby test 20 concepts per week instead of 2. Worth a conversation?

## 6. Gismart
**Location:** London, UK (HQ), Minsk, Belarus (dev center)

**Focus:** Music games, idle games, hyper-casual (Beat Maker Go, Cool Goal)  
**Funding:** $15M+ raised, Series A  
**Key Contact:** Mykola Tymkiv, CEO  
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/mykola-tymkiv  
**Verification:** High (Crunchbase, press coverage)

**Live Ops Focus:** Weekly content drops, seasonal events, influencer collaborations

**Outreach Angle:**  
> Mykola—Gismart's music games require constant content updates (new beats, instruments, effects). We've built AI that generates royalty-free music loops and sound effects—could cut your audio production costs by 60% while increasing output. Curious if this aligns with Gismart's content strategy?

- Multiple public sources (Crunchbase, press, LinkedIn)
